title: Creative Marketing Director
description: CONFIDENTIAL EXECUTIVE SEARCH Company identity and compensation details will be disclosed to shortlisted candidates only. All applications and conversations are held in strict confidence. Creative Marketing Director The Opportunity Our client, a fast-scaling DTC health and telehealth company, is conducting a confidential search for a Creative Marketing Director to lead and personally shape the creative engine behind its paid performance marketing. This is an executive-level, high-impact mandate for a proven creative leader who combines strategic vision with elite hands-on craft — someone who can set direction at the leadership table in the morning and be inside Figma or a video editor by the afternoon. This is not a delegate-and-review position. The successful candidate will own the creative process end-to-end — strategy, production, and optimization — across Meta, Google, TikTok, YouTube, Reddit, and emerging channels, while personally producing best-in-class work and building the function that will scale beneath them. You will report directly to the co-founders and hold full ownership of the customer's creative journey — from the hook that stops the scroll to the landing page that closes the sale. Mandate & Scope Creative Production & Craft (60%) Personally direct and produce a high-volume slate of ad variants — 15–30 per week — spanning static, short-form video, and copy, setting the creative bar for the organization. Lead direct-response copywriting and creative direction: hooks, headlines, body copy, and calls to action engineered to convert, not merely to impress. Bring an exceptional, refined design sensibility — native command of Figma or equivalent, and fluency in agile short-form video editing — producing work that is both commercially effective and visually distinguished. Operate as an AI-native creative leader, embedding tools such as ChatGPT, Claude, Midjourney, RunwayML, and ElevenLabs into ideation, production, and scaling workflows as a core operating capability. Strategy & Performance Optimization (25%) Set and own creative strategy across Meta, Google, TikTok, YouTube, and Reddit, calibrating format and approach to what each platform currently rewards. Govern performance through daily data review — CTR, CPA, ROAS, CVR, hook rate — with clear authority to kill, scale, or iterate. Architect a structured testing framework across hooks, formats, audiences, and offers, replacing gut instinct with a repeatable system. Maintain a leading-edge view of the DTC health and telehealth landscape, anticipating hook fatigue and shifting compliance dynamics ahead of competitors. Conversion & Full-Funnel Leadership (10%) Audit the complete path from ad to checkout, ensuring creative promise and post-click experience form a single, coherent system. Bring CRO-informed recommendations on landing page layout, copy, and CTA design, backed by data. Shape offer presentation — pricing, urgency, and objection handling — as a core creative and commercial lever. Organizational Leadership & Growth (5%) Partner directly with the co-founders to align creative output with growth targets and unit economics. Lay the foundation for the creative function's future: playbooks, systems, and — as the business scales — a team of freelancers and junior creatives reporting into this role. Candidate Profile Required Based in the United States. A track record as a builder, not just a director — recent roles must reflect genuine hands-on production, not oversight alone. This leader still opens Figma, writes the copy, and ships the ad personally. A genuine, well-developed artistic and editorial eye, grounded in formal or deeply practiced design and copywriting craft — typography, visual hierarchy, and color theory held to the same standard as CPA and ROAS. A portfolio demonstrating direct-response results — measurable conversion outcomes, not brand impressions or engagement alone. Deep, current, hands-on fluency with Meta Ads Manager, Google Ads, TikTok A
score_reason: Senior creative leadership with hands-on scope and DTC expertise, but no explicit remote confirmation, no compensation disclosed, and performance marketing focus is narrower than Travis's integrated campaign background.

title: Head of Performance Creative
description: Location: Remote About the Company This company is a fast-growing supplement brand focused on absorption and bioavailability. Most supplements are not designed for the body to absorb them, which wastes the majority of the nutrients people take. This brand uses proprietary technology and clinical-grade ingredients in their most bioavailable form to increase absorption rates by up to 10x. From deep sleep support to rapid hydration and immunity, the powdered supplement lineup delivers nutrients directly into the body with results people can actually feel. Role Overview As Head of Performance Creative , you will lead the strategy, execution, and optimization of all direct response creative across paid social, TikTok Shop, creator content, and emerging acquisition channels. This is a performance creative role first and foremost. You will partner closely with media buying, creators, and leadership to build a best-in-class performance creative operation, and you will establish scalable systems for briefing, scripting, testing, reporting, and optimization. What You'll Do Creative strategy and performance: Lead the direct response creative strategy across Meta, TikTok, TikTok Shop, YouTube, and other paid acquisition channels, building scalable testing frameworks that improve CTR, hook rate, hold rate, CAC, MER, and overall acquisition efficiency. Creative testing and iteration: Own the end-to-end creative testing pipeline, identifying winning angles, messaging frameworks, hooks, formats, and offers, and translating performance data into concrete creative iterations and scaling decisions. Partnership and whitelisted ads: Bring real fluency in partnership ads and whitelisted ads, understanding what creative performs when it runs through influencer and creator accounts even though sourcing influencers sits outside this role. Attribution and analytics: Use attribution and reporting platforms such as Northbeam or an equivalent, plus platform-native analytics, to evaluate creative performance and guide a rigorous, data-first approach to optimization and scaling. Scriptwriting and creative direction: Write and oversee high-performing ad scripts, creator briefs, UGC frameworks, and messaging concepts built for conversion-focused paid social, keeping everything on brand voice while maximizing performance. Agency and vendor management: Oversee relationships with external creative agencies and production partners, auditing deliverables, reviewing briefs, and keeping external teams aligned with performance goals and brand expectations. Cross functional collaboration: Work closely with growth, influencer, TikTok Shop, and leadership teams to align creative strategy with business objectives, product launches, and customer acquisition priorities. Who You Are 5+ years in DTC ecommerce, performance marketing, or paid social creative strategy, ideally within health, wellness, supplements, or consumer brands. Proven experience scaling performance creative across Meta, TikTok, and creator-led acquisition channels. Real fluency in partnership ads and whitelisting, with a clear understanding of what makes influencer-run creative convert. Comfortable working in Northbeam or a similar attribution platform, with a deep understanding of creative analytics and media buying performance metrics. Strong scripting and messaging skills, with a track record of writing high-converting direct response creative. Highly analytical, with the ability to spot trends, diagnose creative fatigue, and scale winning concepts efficiently. Excellent communication and project management skills with strong attention to detail. Comfortable operating in a fast-moving environment with aggressive performance goals and quick turnaround expectations.
score_reason: Senior remote role with strong performance creative scope, but DTC/supplement focus and performance metrics emphasis differs from Travis's integrated campaign heritage.

title: Head of Brand & Marketing
description: We don't need another marketer. We need someone who makes people stop scrolling. Someone who turns customers into fans. Followers into advocates. Ideas into movements. If your first instinct when you see a campaign is to ask, "How do we make this unforgettable?" —keep reading. About Plastic Bank We're not trying to become another sustainability company. We're building one of the world's most recognized impact brands. Plastic Bank helps companies transform plastic waste into measurable environmental and social impact. We work with some of the world's leading brands and operate across multiple continents—but we're just getting started. The opportunity isn't to maintain our brand. It's to build what comes next. We're Looking for Someone with Great Taste. Taste isn't listed on a résumé. It can't be measured by years of experience. And you won't earn it from another marketing certification. It's the instinct to know when something is memorable—and when it's just...fine. It's recognizing that the best ideas are often the simplest. It's pushing for work that people want to share, not because they have to, but because they genuinely love it. It's obsessing over the details, challenging ordinary thinking, and refusing to settle for average. We believe great brands aren't built by bigger budgets. They're built by people with curiosity, courage, and exceptional creative judgment. If that sounds like you, keep reading. What You'll Own You'll own the entire marketing function. You'll lead an incredible team of creative, content, design, and production professionals. Your job isn't to create every asset. Your job is to inspire better ideas, raise the creative bar, and help the team execute work that people actually remember. You'll help shape our voice. You'll influence our product launches. You'll partner with Sales. You'll challenge assumptions. You'll meet with customers. You'll speak at events. You'll build executive thought leadership. You'll create campaigns people want to share. Most importantly... You'll help make Plastic Bank impossible to ignore. What Success Looks Like One year from now... People know who Plastic Bank is. Customers engage with us because they genuinely want to. Our social channels feel alive. Our brand has a distinct personality. Our stories travel further. Our marketing generates conversations—not just impressions. The team is producing work they're proud of. And marketing has become one of the company's greatest competitive advantages. We Think You'll Love This Role If... You have notebooks full of ideas. You screenshot great marketing. You rewrite headlines in your head. You notice branding everywhere. You get excited about building communities. You believe B2B marketing shouldn't feel like B2B marketing. You think sustainability deserves better storytelling. You love mentoring creative people. You enjoy rolling up your sleeves and making ideas happen. You Might Be a Great Fit If... You're confident in leading marketing for a growing company. You've built a brand—not just managed one. You've grown engaged communities, not just follower counts. You've led creative teams and know how to get the best from designers, writers, and content creators. You're comfortable presenting to executives, customers, and partners. You balance creativity with business results. You're just as comfortable brainstorming a campaign as you are reviewing performance metrics. Bonus Points You have B2B or SaaS experience. You have a strong understanding of digital marketing You've worked in sustainability, social impact, or mission-driven organizations. You've built executive thought leadership. You have an eye for exceptional creative. You know that great brands are built through consistency, not one viral moment. Screening Process We want to ensure that all applicants have a fair and equal chance, so we’re using a TestGorilla assessment. This minimizes unconscious bias in our hiring process. Successful candidates will be invited to a j

title: Senior Brand Writer (Temp-to-Hire)

Named one of Fast Company's Most Innovative Companies and to the CNBC Disruptor 50, Armada’s solutions are deployed in over 60 countries globally for organizations ranging from energy to defense. With nearly $500 million in funding to date, Armada is backed by leading investors including Founders Fund, Lux, BlackRock and Microsoft (M12), alongside strategic partnerships with Microsoft, Dell, Palantir, NVIDIA, SpaceX, and Skydio. We are building the infrastructure layer for sovereign and edge AI - rugged, deployable compute for customers that cannot rely on centralized cloud. Working at Armada means taking ownership, driving autonomy, and delivering impact. You’ll tackle challenges that haven’t been solved before and help build something transformative from the ground up. What you do here will not only define your career but help further Armada’s mission to bridge the digital divide for customers around the world. About the Role Armada is looking for a Senior Brand Writer to shape how the company sounds across every surface where words matter. Reporting to the Head of Communications, this is a craft role for someone who can write a five-word headline that lands, then turn around and write a thousand words of balanced, on-brand product copy. You will own the voice of the brand and partner closely with creative, product, and executive leadership to bring it to life. The ideal candidate echoes Armada’s quiet confidence. Restraint over flourish. Specificity over abstraction. A point of view that doesn’t need to raise its voice. You understand intuitively what separates real writing from AI-generated content. You will go deep on our products, learn the technical substance, and translate it into words that customers, partners, and prospects want to stop and read. Above all, you know how to find the human story inside a complex product. Our customers are people solving hard problems in hard places, and their pain points are what bring our products to life on the page. Please note that this role is a temp-to-hire position. Strong performers will be considered for full-time placement at the end of the contract period. This role is eligible for overtime. Location: This role is office-based in our Bellevue, WA or San Francisco, CA locations, or remote in Los Angeles, CA. What You'll Do (Key Responsibilities) Write razor-sharp headlines, taglines, and brand-level copy that define how Armada shows up in the world Develop long-form product narratives, web copy, and marketing collateral that translate complex edge computing and AI infrastructure into clear, compelling stories Shape the narrative of sales and investor decks Draft press releases, executive communications, and other earned media materials in support of Armada’s communications strategy Support social channels with original writing that maintains brand voice across formats Collaborate with designers, product managers, and engineers to ensure copy and design are developed in lockstep, not as separate handoffs Develop and maintain Armada’s brand identity, and help others across the company write in our voice Required Qualifications 8+ years of copy writing experience in brand, marketing, editorial, or agency settings Exceptional portfolio demonstrating range across headlines, long-form product copy, decks, and editorial writing Sharp instinct for what to cut. You know when one word does the work of five A clear point of view on what good writing is, paired with the humility to take feedback and make it better An excellent listener who can absorb raw input from stakeholders and shape it into a story with a clear through-line Range. You can shift registers when the brand or the moment calls for

title: Vice President, Brand Strategy
description: Vice President (VP) — Brand Strategy Position Overview The Vice President (VP) of Brand Strategy is responsible for the overall planning, development, and management of the company's brand strategy, as well as defining and driving the brand's long-term direction. The role encompasses brand positioning, value proposition development, market strategy, brand communication, consumer insights, brand equity management, and global brand expansion. The VP ensures the establishment of a globally competitive jewelry brand image while enhancing brand influence, market awareness, and commercial value. Key Responsibilities I. Brand Strategic Planning and Development Formulate medium- to long-term brand development strategies; Define brand positioning, the brand value system, and market development direction; Optimize the strategic brand layout and drive international brand expansion; Establish brand growth pathways aligned with corporate development goals. II. Brand Positioning and Market Strategy Formulate brand positioning and market competition strategies; Conduct in-depth analysis of industry trends, consumer needs, and the competitive landscape; Drive increased brand awareness in both domestic and international markets; Develop growth strategies for the high-end jewelry market and target customer segments. III. Brand Communication and Marketing Management Formulate brand communication strategies and annual brand promotion plans; Coordinate online and offline brand marketing activities; Manage brand storytelling, visual identity, and communication content; Enhance brand exposure, market influence, and consumer loyalty. IV. Brand Equity and Partnership Management Establish and maintain a long-term brand equity management system; Develop and manage strategic partnerships, media relations, and industry resources; Drive brand collaborations, cross-sector partnerships, and international cooperation projects; Enhance the brand's commercial value and industry influence. V. Cross-functional Strategic Collaboration Coordinate with marketing, sales, product, design, and operations teams; Ensure brand strategy alignment with the company's overall business strategy; Integrate brand philosophy into product development, customer experience, and business operations; Establish standardized brand management processes to improve execution efficiency. VI. Brand Analysis and Growth Management Analyze market trends, consumer behavior, and brand data; Formulate brand growth strategies and optimize brand development models; Evaluate brand performance and market feedback; Enhance brand competitiveness, customer conversion rates, and long-term profitability. VII. Brand Risk and Compliance Management Identify potential risks during brand building and market promotion; Ensure brand communications comply with relevant laws, regulations, and industry standards; Safeguard brand reputation and market image; Ensure the brand's long-term, stable development. Qualifications 20–25 years of professional experience in brand strategy, brand management, marketing, or related fields; Over 8 years of experience in senior management roles (Director level or above); Familiarity with brand operations in the jewelry, luxury goods, or high-end consumer goods sectors is preferred; Experience in international brand building, global marketing, or cross-border brand management is preferred; Excellent capabilities in brand strategic planning, market insight, communication and coordination, and execution. We Offer Competitive executive compensation and benefits packages; Opportunities for long-term career development and advancement; The opportunity to serve as a core management team member of a rapidly growing international jewelry enterprise; The opportunity to play a key role in formulating global brand strategy, brand building, and international expansion.
score_reason: Senior brand strategy role with global scope, but no mention of remote work, creative leadership, or campaign execution—reads more strategy/corporate than creative direction.

title: SVP, Brand & Marketing
description: Overview Of The Company Job Description FOX Television Stations (FTS) is seeking a new executive leadership position to unify and elevate our brand and marketing functions across our portfolio of stations and streaming platforms. The SVP, Brand & Marketing will be the senior creative and marketing leader for FTS, owning the visual identity, creative production and audience growth strategy for one of the largest local media groups in the country; which includes our 29 broadcast stations, FOX Local, and LiveNOW from FOX. This role serves as the senior creative and marketing partner to station leadership across all FTS markets, leading the groupwide Creative & Graphic Production Hub and Marketing & Growth team. The position equips local teams with the strategy, tools, resources, and guidance needed to drive marketing performance, grow audiences, and deepen engagement with viewers and users. IS THIS YOU? You always put the viewer first. Every creative decision, every campaign, every workflow improvement is evaluated through the lens of whether it makes the experience better for the people consuming your content. You thrive in a complex, multi-platform environment and know how to build systems and structures that make creative work scalable. You are as comfortable presenting to a room of executives as you are diving into a campaign brief or reviewing a motion graphics package. You see marketing as an opportunity to innovate and connect, not simply a mechanism to push viewers toward a product. You are as creative in how you solve problems as you are in how you design brands and marketing strategies. You believe great content is often the most powerful marketing tool available, and that a talent's authentic presence can do more for a brand than any traditional promo. title: Lead Forward Deployed Creative Technologist
description: The Opportunity Every global brand is now a content factory — thousands of on-brand variations a quarter across static, video, and 3D, shipped in days not weeks. We embed with enterprise customers, learn their content supply chain end-to-end, and build the custom AI-powered workflows that get them there — on Adobe Firefly Services, Firefly Creative Production (our node-based workflow builder), and Adobe Brand Intelligence (ABI) for brand validation and compliance. Here's what most people miss: by the time you arrive, the deal is signed — but the people who have to use what we sold never signed up for it. They're creatives and marketers with tools they already like, and nobody asked them. You can't mandate adoption — you earn it: find the users with a real pain, build something so clearly better they reach for it on their own, and make it the way their team works. Usage that repeats at scale is the only proof that counts — and it's what makes an enterprise customer renew. We are not pre-sales. We are not consultants. We are technical creators who ship — and who get used. What you'll do Find the real users and the use cases that stick. The signed deal tells you what the buyer agreed to buy — not who'll actually use it, or which use cases earn repeat usage. That's yours to discover. Land an undeniable first win, fast. Compose Firefly Services APIs, Workflow Builder nodes, and ABI checks into something that runs — ship ugly first, and meet users inside the tools they already live in (a Figma plugin, a Premiere panel, their DAM), not a new destination. Drive it to repeat, at-scale use. You don't convert a 200-person creative org one desk at a time — you run the workshop, the live build, the training that makes them want to switch. Then integrate with their stack (AEM, Workfront, Frame.io, brand portal), own the edge cases, and hand off so they self-serve. Clear the blockers, wherever they are. Diagnose what's really stopping adoption — a product gap, a missing integration, a blocking dependency. Build the workaround when the product can't do it yet; take the real gaps upstream and influence the roadmap or commit the fix yourself; chase a cross-team dependency until it moves. You don't file a ticket and wait. Make the next engagement faster. Reusable patterns become shared templates and upstream Workflow Builder nodes. Every win compounds. Demo your own work — script, produce, edit. Real outcomes, not slides. What you'll bring The bar — what we filter hardest for: You earn adoption against resistance. You've won over the user who didn't want your tool (they had their own), found another way in when a stakeholder went dark, and turned a roomful of skeptics into users through the workshop or live build. You measure yourself in adoption and renewals, not features shipped. You build it yourself. Thin front-ends, light back-ends (App Builder, Lambda/Functions), custom nodes, integrations — a working URL the customer uses this quarter. The floor — we don't teach this: Creative content supply-chain craft. Briefs to assets, brand systems and design tokens, masters to channel variants, the Figma / PSD / INDD → DAM → review-and-approval handoff. A skeptical creative director trusts you in the first meeting. A public portfolio (link or PDF at application) — shipped creative and technical work. Hard requirement; no portfolio, no review. Force multipliers — real, but teachable: Firefly Services, Firefly Creative Production, and Adobe Brand Intelligence fluency — or proof you ramp a new API surface in days. Adobe ecosystem (AEM, Workfront, Frame.io, App Builder) and Figma plugin/API/MCP depth are pluses. AI-native building with judgment. Cursor / Claude Code / Copilot daily — fast, but you catch when the output is wrong. You explore the frontier (ComfyUI, n8n, LangGraph, MCPs) to stay sharp. High EQ, low ego. 5–10 years straddling creative production and technical building. Travel 10–20%. What we're not looking for The order-taker. You shipped th

Une communication fluide et claire au sein d'une équipe collaborative à distance. Atouts (Points bonis) Expérience sur des titres publiés en VR / XR (Quest ou PCVR) et un instinct pour le confort, l'échelle et la lisibilité en réalité virtuelle. Expérience avec Unity et les pipelines de rendu stylisé/NPR Un amour pour la direction artistique "à la Nintendo" : l'intemporel au-delà de la tendance, la clarté plutôt que le désordre. De l'enthousiasme pour les flux de travail assistés par l'IA en tant que multiplicateur de force (nous en tirons parti). The team ships ~15 ads/week today; your job is to get us to 25+ per week without quality dropping. - Weekly readouts — diagnose winners and losers, turn results into iteration hypotheses and next bets. - Creator + editor quality bar — brief UGC creators and celebrity talent so raw footage arrives right the first time; review final briefs and final edits so the intended creative survives to the cut. - Experimentation — Overarching and persona-driven monthly experiments that expand our reach. - Mentorship — mentor and level up our junior creative strategists toward independent ownership; you're their creative sparring partner in the weekly sync and sprints. - AI leverage — use AI tooling across research, scripting, and variant production to scale output without scaling headcount. Bonus Points - Gaming, esports, sports, fantasy, or other regulated-adjacent audiences - You've bought media yourself (Meta/TikTok Ads Manager literacy) - Mobile app UA context - You've worked with celebrity-level talent Compensation & Logistics - $125k salary + benefits + equity - Full-time, remote to start with US-hours overlap; option to relocate / join the NYC office later - Reports to the Head of Marketing

description: About Fello: Fello is a profitable, hyper-growth, VC-backed B2B SaaS company building the agentic real estate platform of the future. Our platform combines data intelligence, marketing automation, and conversational AI to help real estate teams engage smarter and scale faster. At the center of it is Felix, our AI teammate, which takes real work off a team's plate — running follow-up across calls, texts, and emails today, with more of the team's day-to-day work coming next. We're not another tool in the stack. We're the system that powers it — learning, adapting, and executing in real time so teams can focus on growth, not guesswork. If you're excited about building an AI product that's changing how an entire industry works, you'll fit right in. About You: You are a strategic, hands-on product marketing leader who knows how to turn product, customer insight, market context, and company strategy into a clear story the whole business can use. You are excellent at positioning and messaging. You can cite examples of how you’ve applied strategic narrative frameworks. You know how to simplify complex products without dumbing them down. You can turn AI, data, CRM, automation, and workflow complexity into buyer value, field confidence, and customer clarity. You are a great writer — and you actually like writing. You will produce and review a lot of copy in this role: web, deck, launch, enablement, and customer-facing. You pair that craft with real strategic judgment, and you collaborate well across departments — an exceptional communicator who can work across founders, executives, Product, Sales, Customer Success, Marketing, Enablement, and RevOps, influencing without authority and keeping the company from fragmenting as product velocity increases. This is not a manager-first role. You will guide existing and future PMM resources, and may build a small team over time — but your sleeves are rolled up every day. You write. You synthesize. You build. You ship. You are a quarterback on the field, not a coach on the sidelines. You are also AI-native in how you work. You use AI to move faster, research smarter, analyze customer and sales signals, and operate like a larger PMM function before adding headcount. And you will have leverage most PMMs never get: a Technical Product Marketing Manager as a close collaborator on deep product work, a GTM AI team that builds agentic solutions alongside you, and an AI agent that understands everything about the product. One more thing about timing: we are at the moment where the story gets set. The next two quarters of positioning and narrative work will matter more than the next two years. You should find that energizing. You Will: Drive the Fello + Felix story Drive the core positioning, messaging, buyer narrative, and product story for Fello and Felix — co-owning the strategic narrative with our President, who holds final approval — and keep it consistent as the product, market, and commercial motion evolve. Translate Fello’s category and strategic narrative work into clear GTM language across the website, sales deck, demo narrative, launch materials, customer proof, and enablement. Work closely with our Technical Product Marketing Manager — a collaborator, not a direct report — on deep product, data, and integration storytelling. Define the language Fello uses — and just as importantly, the language we avoid. Make complex product capabilities easy for customers, Sales, CS, and Marketing to understand and use. Turn product velocity into market absorption Own launch and release readiness across GTM teams: launch briefs, release tiering, internal communications, field enablement inputs, customer messaging, and post-launch feedback. Make sure every meaningful product change is translated into why it matters, who it matters for, how we explain it, and what teams need to do next. Partner closely with Product on what is shipping, what changed, and what the field needs to know — and build the feedback loop

You have recruited and onboarded the founding game development team, maintaining regular, high-quality release cycles. title: 100x Marketer - Chief Marketing Officer
description: From Zeb Evans, CEO We're executing on the 100x org. I'm looking for ONE person to own the entire brand marketing function at ClickUp. Not manage a team of 50. Not sit in meetings all day. Not write briefs for other people to execute. ONE PERSON who builds the systems that produce world-class marketing at scale, and reviews everything with taste sharp enough to know the difference between great and garbage. We call this person an Artisan . What is an Artisan? An Artisan is not just "a good marketer." An Artisan is someone whose taste and judgment are so refined that they can orchestrate AI systems at hyperspeed while maintaining quality that lesser teams can't achieve at any speed. Taste: They know what good looks like before it exists. They can evaluate 50 AI-generated options and immediately identify the 2 worth publishing. They've internalized the craft so deeply that quality assessment is instantaneous. Judgment: They know what to build, not just how. They understand the audience, the market, and the tradeoffs well enough to make high-stakes calls without committees. Speed of orchestration: They don't just "prompt AI." They operate AI systems like instruments: fast iteration loops, rapid evaluation, continuous course correction. What takes a team of 15 six weeks takes an Artisan days. Ownership breadth: An Artisan owns a surface end to end. Not "the social part" or "the email part." The WHOLE thing: strategy, systems, execution, quality. Fewer people means fewer handoffs. Fewer handoffs means faster, better output. This is the marketing equivalent of what our 10x Engineers do: own an entire product surface, idea to execution to live. The only two things that matter 01: Systems - Can you build marketing systems that compound? Not "use ChatGPT sometimes." BUILD and ITERATE on the SYSTEM. AI agents that produce campaigns. Workflows that generate and distribute content. Automation that handles email, social, lifecycle at scale. Systems that get better every single week because they learn from the feedback loop YOU create. The output should compound without you manually touching every piece. 02: Review - Can you judge quality at an absurdly high bar? When the system produces something mid, can you reject it in a way that makes the system LEARN? Can you look at 50 pieces of output and immediately know which 2 are worth publishing? Your taste IS the product. The systems do the volume. You do the judgment. And when you reject something, that rejection feeds back into the system so it never makes the same mistake twice. What You Own Brand messaging and positioning for everything ClickUp puts into the world Campaign strategy, creative direction, and the systems that execute them Social media presence, content, and engagement Email marketing, drip campaigns, and lifecycle flows Content production pipelines across all formats Quality gates: nothing ships unless it meets your bar You build systems that do it. You are the taste. The agents are the hands. Why this is the best marketing job on earth ClickUp is a $4B company growing fast, with 3,000+ internal AI agents already running. Our product IS the convergence of context. You have TOTAL CONTEXT on everything happening in the company, available to your AI systems. We're building a 100x software framework where you can market literally ANY product, ANY solution. AI products, app solutions, platform capabilities, vertical packages. The surface area is massive. The creative canvas is limitless. You'll have: Direct reporting to the CEO (me). No layers. No politics. An infinite product surface to market: AI, apps, agents, solutions, platform Access to every piece of company context for your AI systems $1M comp growth path The most interesting AI-native marketing challenge that exists right now No gates. Only signal. No years-of-experience requirement No degree required No resume needed CMO title available based on the person Only criteria: Systems + Review. That's it. How To Apply No resum

title: Creative Director

Unlike other social media agencies or YouTube businesses, Dragonfruit refuses to celebrate a “creator economy” that trades viewers’ time and attention for rapid-fire dopamine entertainment. Instead, we want to lead a global movement to make YouTube a more valuable and educational place for all people; where views and subscribers are never an end in and of themselves, but rather a means of increasing human flourishing; where viral-educational YouTube channels not only bolster awareness and impact for our client's brands, but also create genuine value in viewers’ lives. Dragonfruit's Mission Dragonfruit exists to help the world’s most important ideas travel farther. We partner with experts, educators, scientists, founders, and creators to turn deep knowledge into media people actually want to watch, share, and act on. In a world where attention is often optimized for noise, we’re building a company that rewards clarity, substance, and human impact — combining creative judgment, production craft, and AI-powered systems to help better information reach more people. Our mission is to build the creative infrastructure for a healthier, more thoughtful internet. Day-to-day you will collaborate with YouTube Producers, Post-Production Managers, video editors, and thumbnail designers, and consult with other Creative Directors and team leads, to design, build, and optimize powerful, educational YouTube brands. From competitor research, to brand positioning, art direction, concept ideation, title/thumbnail design, script optimization, final video edit reviews, studying analytics, running a/b tests, and swapping out packaging after publication, you will have the opportunity to create life-changing growth for your clients and their viewers. What you can expect: An exciting startup culture – work cross-functionally, wear several hats, and occasionally work long hours, during evenings, and/or weekends. title: Directeur·trice de création | Creative Director
description: Directeur·trice de création La vie chez Bloom Rejoignez l’équipe dynamique de Bloom, une agence numérique innovante avec des bureaux à Montréal et Toronto. Ici, on place la flexibilité, le bien-être de nos employés et l’équilibre entre vie professionnelle et personnelle au cœur de nos priorités. Depuis 19 ans, nous sommes fiers d’être reconnus comme l’un des meilleurs employeurs au Canada. Avec plus de 75 spécialistes passionnés, nous aidons les marques à se démarquer en ligne grâce à une approche marketing 360. Bloom offre un modèle de travail hybride, et nos entrevues se déroulent par vidéoconférence. Certains postes peuvent nécessiter des rencontres en personne. Aperçu du rôle Bloom accélère sa croissance en déployant un produit créatif ancré à la fois dans la performance et l'excellence artisanale (craft). Nous sommes à la recherche d'un·e directeur·trice de création pour définir et guider cette évolution. Il s'agit d'un rôle de leadership clé dans lequel vous façonnerez le modèle créatif, l'équipe et le travail lui-même. Vous comprenez comment les idées voyagent à travers la culture et comment les outils émergents transforment la production créative. Vous définirez la présence de nos clients sur toutes les plateformes, ancrerez les campagnes dans des idées fortes et veillerez à ce que notre production créative capte l'attention du public cible tout en générant des résultats mesurables. Cela inclut l'intégration responsable et intentionnelle d'outils génératifs et intelligents pour renforcer à la fois l'art de la création et la performance. Responsabilités clés Vision créative et excellence de l'exécution (Craftsmanship) : Assumer la responsabilité des standards créatifs pour l'ensemble des projets clients, en dirigeant le développement des idées de campagne, des systèmes de contenu, de la direction artistique et de la conception-rédaction. Agir en tant qu'autorité créative finale pour garantir une qualité irréprochable et réduire les cycles de révision. Résultats axés sur la performance : Propulser un contenu créatif optimisé pour des plateformes comme Meta, TikTok et Google. Travailler en étroite collaboration avec les équipes de médias payants et de stratégie, en prônant une approche d'expérimentation (test-and-learn) pour préserver l'intégrité créative tout en maximisant les résultats mesurables. Intégration de l'IA générative : Apporter une expérience pratique des outils génératifs (image, vidéo, texte) afin d'enrichir l'idéation et la production. Collaborer avec la direction de l'agence pour établir des directives claires d'utilisation responsable, naviguer les questions éthiques et encadrer l'équipe dans une application efficace et critique. Leadership client et d'équipe : Présenter et vendre le travail avec clarté aux parties prenantes de direction, en instaurant une relation de confiance et en guidant les clients vers des décisions créatives plus audacieuses. Gérer, diriger, encadrer et perfectionner les designers et les créateurs, en élevant la barre tant pour l'aspect visuel que pour la rédaction au sein de toute l'équipe. Bâtir le moteur créatif : Contribuer à optimiser et à concevoir des systèmes évolutifs et structurés (briefing, idéation, assurance qualité) qui équilibrent l'ambition créative et la faisabilité technique. Participer à l'évolution globale du produit créatif et du positionnement de Bloom. Connaissances et compétences Un·e leader créatif·ve accompli·e possédant de 8 à 12 ans (ou plus) d'expérience en agence et un portfolio démontrant l'excellence dans le développement de concepts, le récit de marque (storytelling), la direction artistique et la conception-rédaction. Aussi à l'aise pour discuter de vision créative, d'objectifs d'affaires que de détails d'exécution, avec la capacité de lier ces trois éléments pour créer un travail à impact mesurable. Expérience dans la mise en place et l'amélioration de systèmes créatifs, de flux de travail (workflows) et de normes de qualité permettant aux équipe
score_reason: Senior CD role in Canada with creative leadership scope, but hybrid model (not 100% remote) and no USD compensation listed are notable gaps.

We're hiring the person who owns all of it: the brand , the media machine, the community , and the affiliate engine that turns belief into sales. WarriorBabe YouTube becomes the flagship: a connected-story slate, series architecture, and spin-off shows per voice where they earn it. Our podcast gets a strategic rename and repositioning (you make that call). The affiliate army. Design and launch a TikTok Shop affiliate program with recruitment, activation, and leaderboard mechanics strong enough to compete at category-benchmark scale (think: 13,000+ affiliates, tens of millions in affiliate-driven revenue). The drop machine. Build and prove a repeatable playbook for hyped product drops — reverse-engineered from affiliates × activation rate × units, plus owned-audience conversion — building toward 50,000 units sold in 24 hours. Community. Design and pilot a named community program, pop-up events, and a city-ambassador model — the layer that turns audience into belonging, and belonging into commerce. The department. Build the org from the ground up: a Brand Voice Lead, an Organic Lead, and three new hires you'll recruit — Studio Lead, Affiliate & Commerce Lead, and Community & Events Lead — plus a shared production bench underneath. Who you are You're a brand-and-community builder with commerce receipts — not a paid-media direct-response operator, not an agency brand strategist. We have a strong preference for female talent: we're a woman-led company serving women, and the strongest candidates for this seat have usually lived this demo or built for it. description: About Us Hyprwork is a fast-growing operator that builds and scales direct-to-consumer brands in health and wellness. Our flagship brand, Rejuvacare, proved the model, with products like RejuvaKnee that help adults reclaim mobility and live with less pain, trusted by over 400,000 customers and shipping primarily to the United States. The Role This is a senior role that owns the strategic brand and product vision across our brands, with the focus now on the newer brands in the portfolio. It reports to the CEO, and you will build the team around it. Our brand challenge is claims-led, not product-led. The outcome we promise, real relief and better living, is not visually self-evident the way a fashion or shapewear product is. A shapewear brand can let the product speak for itself; we cannot. Our brand has to build belief through claim, mechanism, and story, and it has to do that within compliance. A strong fashion or product-led track record does not automatically transfer to this problem. You will own brand positioning, narrative, voice, and identity, and the vision extends into product itself: digital product, including claims and UX and UI at a directional level, physical product and the packaging experience, social media, and the UGC that increasingly carries our brands. This is brand and product vision that performs and sells, not brand for its own sake. What You Will Own Brand foundation. Owning positioning, narrative, and the messaging architecture across the portfolio, with a focus on the newer brands. Identity and guidelines. Owning brand voice, visual identity, and tone, and the guidelines that hold them consistent as the company scales. Product vision. Directional ownership of the product experience: digital product including claims and UX and UI at a surface level, physical product, and the packaging experience. Social and UGC. Steering social media presence and the UGC engine so the brand shows up consistently and credibly where customers actually are. Brand meets performance. Partnering with performance and creative so brand and direct response reinforce each other, and claims are built and tested responsibly and compliantly. Team and brand health. Building and leading the brand team over time, and tracking brand health so the customer's perception informs how we build. Requirements What We Are Looking For A senior brand or brand-and-product leader for DTC consumer brands, ideally health, wellness, supplements, or other claims-led categories, with the maturity to build and lead a team. A track record building brand and product vision from a low base: positioning, messaging, identity, and the connection into product, packaging, and social, not just maintaining an existing brand book. A real grasp of claims-led health and wellness: how to build belief through claim and mechanism within compliance, and why a strong fashion or product-led track record does not automatically transfer. Comfort operating inside a performance and direct-response environment, where brand is expected to drive conversion, not only to build awareness. Native or near-native English, with the editorial and aesthetic judgment to own brand voice for a US audience and to represent the brand to US media and partners. Nice to Have Experience in health and wellness, supplements, or the 55-plus demographic, a creator or influencer ecosystem background, packaging and product-marketing experience, and experience launching new brands from zero. Benefits What We Offer A fully remote leadership position with real ownership over a function you will build and lead, and direct visibility with the founders. A monthly performance bonus tied to your KPIs, on top of base. Paid time off.

Who we are Squido Studio is a small, remote-first Québec studio of unapologetic game lovers and VR believers. We've shipped titles like No More Rainbows , DigiGods , and Wizherd , and we run a flat, no-BS shop where transparency is our #1 value , we say things as they are, we don't do ego or politics, and everyone's ideas get heard. We're gameplay-first, Nintendo-obsessed, and aggressively curious about new tools (yes, including AI in our pipeline). The art vision We want a Breath of the Wild -inspired art style , vibrant, stylized, cel-shaded, and timeless. Strong readable silhouettes, saturated painterly palettes, soft atmospheric depth, charming character and creature design, and a fully zero-UI, diegetic presentation where the world tells the story. The catch that makes this role special: it all has to look gorgeous and run flawlessly at 72/90fps on standalone VR . In VR, performance is part of the art, and nailing a BotW-caliber look within that budget is exactly the challenge we're hiring for. What you'll own The visual vision. Define and champion the art direction end-to-end, style, color, lighting, character/creature design, environments, VFX, and the zero-UI diegetic language. Build and maintain the art bible the whole team rallies around. The audio vision. Direct music and sound design (in-house and with our audio partners) so the game sounds as intentional as it looks, spatial audio is core to our zero-UI design. Leading the team. Grow and lead a cross-disciplinary art + audio team (concept, 3D environment/character/enemy, VFX, technical art, animation, UI, audio). Set the bar, give sharp and generous feedback, and create an environment where artists do their best work. Beauty within budget. Partner with our Technical Director to hit a stylized visual benchmark that survives standalone-VR constraints, silhouette clarity, stepped toon shading, rim lighting, painterly albedo, baked GI, LODs, and draw-call discipline. Cross-functional partnership. Work hand-in-hand with the Lead Game Designer and engineering to take features from graybox to final quality, and defend a cohesive visual identity across the whole game. Iterate relentlessly. Use playtest feedback to sharpen readability, comfort, and impact, and kill your darlings when the game needs it. What you bring 6+ years in game art , with shipped titles and real time leading/directing an art team. A portfolio that proves strong stylized / non-photorealistic art direction , you can define a cohesive, readable, characterful look (cel-shading, toon/NPR, painterly stylization à la BotW) and hold a team to it. Deep understanding of real-time art performance , you think in draw calls, overdraw, LODs, baked vs. dynamic lighting, and shader cost, not just pretty frames. Leadership people actually want to follow , clear direction, generous feedback, zero ego. You lift the team up rather than gatekeeping. Hands-on fluency , you can jump into the engine/DCC tools to prototype a look, set up a shader/lighting pass, and show rather than just tell. Comfort directing audio (or a strong track record collaborating closely with audio leads/composers). Fluent, clear communication in a collaborative, remote team. Bonus points VR / XR shipped experience (Quest or PCVR) and an instinct for VR comfort, scale, and legibility. Experience with Unity and stylized/NPR rendering pipelines (URP, custom toon shaders, Application SpaceWarp). A love of the Nintendo of art direction, timeless over trendy, clarity over clutter. Enthusiasm for AI-assisted workflows as a force multiplier (we lean into them). Understand and/or speaking french. title: Principal AI Creative Director, POD
description: Description Advertising is being remade. The brands that win the next decade will be the ones that figure out how to deliver creative that is personal, performant, and produced at a scale no human team can match alone. Generative AI is the unlock. But generation alone is not the advantage. The advantage belongs to the system that connects creation to delivery to learning, and gets better with every impression. Amazon Ads is building that system. We are investing in the models, the infrastructure, and the product intelligence to make personalized, brand-aligned creative a default capability for every advertiser, not a luxury reserved for the largest budgets. The work is early, the stakes are high, and the opportunity to define how this category develops is real. We are building that system. We need a Principal AI Creative Director to define what great looks like inside it and to make that definition teachable, scalable, and machine-readable. Key job responsibilities As Principal AI Creative Director for Personalization Optimization and Design (POD), you are the human judgment layer at the center of Amazon's AI creative system. Generative AI has commoditized asset creation. The durable differentiator is now creative direction: the taste, the standards, the brand intelligence, and the systematic thinking that ensures our system produces creative that builds brands, not just optimizes clicks. This is not a role about using AI tools, though you must be among the most fluent practitioners working today. It is about understanding what makes a great commercial, a great piece of content, a great brand moment, and then decomposing that understanding into primitives: the story arc, the color logic, the compositional tension, the emotional beat, the brand signal. Those primitives are what our systems learn from, replicate, and eventually govern at scale. You will operate as a principal-level individual contributor with org-wide influence, partnering directly with design, engineering, science, and product leadership across every surface (POD) owns. A day in the life Creative Direction And Quality Standards Define and own the creative quality bar for AI-generated assets across POD's full portfolio, including images, video, copy, and multi-format compositions. Establish brand intelligence frameworks that ensure AI outputs are aesthetically coherent, emotionally resonant, and brand-safe at scale. Act as the senior creative editor for AI-generated content, distinguishing exceptional work from "good enough" outputs and articulating the difference in decomposable, teachable terms. Decomposing Creative into Primitives. Lead the Systemization of Taste: Break down what makes great creative into its constituent elements: narrative structure, color theory, compositional hierarchy, emotional arc, pacing, and brand signal. Translate these primitives into structured frameworks, including prompt libraries, creative briefs, quality rubrics, and annotation schemas, that inform model training, DCO reward signals, and creative tooling. Define the primitives that power aesthetic moderation: the signals and thresholds that allow our systems to evaluate, filter, and rank AI-generated content without requiring human review of every asset. Partner with applied science and engineering to operationalize these as machine-readable inputs. Hands-On Production And Prototyping Produce high-quality creative content independently across modalities, including image, video, and copy, using the leading AI creation tools. Bring that same hands-on fluency to prototyping: build working creative prototypes that demonstrate new customer experiences, surface trade-offs, and create decision points for design, product, and engineering leadership. This role operates in a demo-driven culture where prototypes move decisions. You are expected to contribute directly to that culture, not just direct others within it. Maintain current, deep fluency across tools including text-to-image (Mid

You are familiar with benchmarking and A/B testing AI outputs. Architectural Efficiency: Minimizing token usage/latency while increasing output quality. Brand Alignment: Scoring of agent outputs against specific brand guidelines using automated evaluation tools. Scale: Successful rollout of core agents used by the entire Pencil user base. Project logistics, budgets, and timelines—managed in service of the story, not at its expense. Post-production quality: editing, motion, sound, color, and versioning across cuts and formats. Beyond craft, they've built creative capability, not just executed against it: scaling teams or distributed creative networks from the ground up, and managing external partners, vendors, and freelance talent to deliver at a level a small in-house team couldn't reach alone. Above all, they bring a relentless, self-directed bias: they go deeper, iterate faster, and don't quit on hard problems. Strong hands-on craft across cameras, lighting, audio, and editing (Premiere, Resolve, or equivalent)—enough to set the standard, even when others hold the camera

description: Contract to start, with opportunity for full-time New York Or Remote (EST Preferred) Contract rate: $90–$135/hr | Full-time base salary: $95,000–$135,000 (base) ‍ We are hiring an art director to lead visual quality, direction, and creative coherence across brand and campaign work. At Little Plains, art direction is the ability to develop a visual point of view for a brand, hold it across executions, and bring others along. This role is responsible for what things look like and why they look that way. You direct and produce work, not just review it. ‍ Why This Role Exists Individual designers do their best work when there is a strong visual direction to work within. Brand guidelines tell you the rules. Art direction tells you why. The companies we work with are often building in visual territory with no obvious references. Good art direction is the difference between a brand that feels like a specific place and one that feels assembled. ‍ About Little Plains Little Plains is a modern brand and product studio. We work primarily with early-stage companies, often from pre-launch through Series A, at moments where clarity, speed, and conviction matter most. Our partners are founders and small senior teams building new products, categories, and narratives under real constraints. We operate as an embedded creative partner rather than a traditional digital agency, building brand, product, and go-to-market work into cohesive systems that can scale as companies grow. Our work spans healthcare, biotech, SaaS, and consumer products. ‍ What You Will Do Day To Day, You Will Lead visual direction for brand and campaign work on your projects Develop visual concepts and art direction from early exploration through final execution Work closely with brand designers to shape, refine, and strengthen the output Define and extend visual systems across multiple touchpoints, not just hero executions Direct photography, illustration, motion, and typography at a concept and execution level Collaborate with brand strategists and copywriters to make sure visual and verbal expression earn each other Use AI image and generative tools to accelerate visual exploration and concept development Hold a high bar for quality and push work forward when it is not there yet ‍ How We Work Art directors at Little Plains are in the work, not above it. Visual concepts are developed through making, not just briefing. Feedback is specific and tied to the intent of the brand, not personal preference. AI is part of the visual exploration process here. The judgment about what to keep or develop is still yours. ‍ The Bar for Craft This role requires a developed visual point of view, the ability to build coherent brand worlds across executions, and the confidence to push work to a higher standard when it does not meet the brief. ‍ What We're Looking For 3–5 years of art direction experience, at an agency, studio, or in-house A portfolio that demonstrates strong visual point of view across brand, campaign, and digital contexts Ability to develop and hold visual direction across a brand system, not just individual executions Comfort directing multiple disciplines: design, photography, illustration, motion Experience working with early-stage companies or building brands from scratch is a plus Active use of AI generative tools (e.g. Flora, Weavy, MidJourney, Claude, Paper) for visual exploration and concept development Strong collaboration skills and the ability to give direct, useful creative feedback ‍ Benefits (Full-Time) Quarterly profit share tied to studio performance Equity participation for long-term team members Medical, dental, and vision insurance (50% employer-covered) 15 days PTO (Year 1, increases by 2 days/year) One week paid office closure every summer Paid office closure between Christmas and New Year's Parental leave: 12 weeks (birth parent), 6 weeks (non-birth parent) at full pay Prenatal leave: 20 hours paid per year $1,000/year craft and tools stipend $200/mont

Be an active face of .monks creatively—presenting work, pitching, and representing us publicly. Help architect the next era of .monks: creative at the center, collaboration at its core. We celebrate diversity and are committed to building a team that reflects the communities we serve. We welcome and encourage qualified applicants, from all backgrounds, who are excited to contribute to our mission. Reporting directly to the Founder, this leader will guide the creative team, establish a stronger point of view for the brand, and elevate creative output across campaigns, content, photography, digital, social, and retail experiences. Key Responsibilities Develop and own the overarching creative vision and brand direction. Partner closely with the Founder to evolve the brand's identity and storytelling. Lead, mentor, and inspire the creative team, including the Managing Art Director, designers, and photographer. Drive concept development for seasonal campaigns, product launches, and brand initiatives. Ensure consistency and excellence across all visual and creative executions. Introduce fresh perspectives, trend awareness, and innovative creative thinking. Balance high-level brand strategy with hands-on creative leadership and feedback. Collaborate cross-functionally with marketing, merchandising, e-commerce, and leadership teams. Ideal Candidate Profile Experienced creative leader with a strong fashion, lifestyle, retail, or consumer brand background. Proven ability to establish a distinctive brand point of view and creative vision. Strong portfolio demonstrating brand-building, campaign development, and visual storytelling. Experience leading and developing multidisciplinary creative teams. Comfortable working directly with founders and executive leadership. Strategic thinker who can elevate the brand while maintaining commercial effectiveness. Willing to travel regularly to Utah and build strong relationships with the in-house team. Salary $125,000-$180,000
score_reason: Senior creative leadership at the right level, but no remote work confirmation, missing salary transparency, and fashion/retail focus is adjacent to Travis's integrated advertising expertise.

title: Creative Director
description: Position Summary Underhill is seeking an experienced Creative Director to support the creative development of an upcoming campaign for a luxury brand within a leading hospitality portfolio. Working alongside Underhill's creative team, the selected candidate will play a key role in shaping the creative direction from concept through production. This role will collaborate directly with both the client and our internal team throughout the development process, helping define the visual language of the campaign before carrying that vision through on set during production. We're looking for someone with a strong background in luxury travel and hospitality , paired with an instinct for authentic lifestyle storytelling. The ideal candidate has experience developing premium campaigns for hotels, resorts, or luxury lifestyle brands, and understands how to create work that feels both elevated and culturally relevant. Please note: Applicants without demonstrated experience creating campaigns for luxury travel or hospitality brands will not be considered. ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Primary Responsibilities & Tasks Collaborate closely with Underhill's founders, internal creative team, and the client to develop the creative vision for the campaign. Help shape the overall visual language, storytelling approach, and creative direction across motion, social and stills. Develop mood boards, references, presentations, and creative treatments that communicate the campaign vision. Work directly with the client throughout the creative process, presenting ideas, incorporating feedback, and building trust. Partner with directors, photographers, and key department heads to ensure the creative vision is achievable and consistent. Attend pre-production meetings, location scouts, and creative reviews as needed. Be an active creative presence during production, ensuring the work captured aligns with the approved vision while adapting to opportunities on set. Review selects and provide creative feedback throughout post-production to maintain consistency from concept through final delivery. Help identify opportunities to elevate the work while balancing creative ambition with production realities. Maintain a strong understanding of luxury hospitality, travel, and lifestyle trends to ensure the work feels contemporary, aspirational, and culturally relevant. ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------- EXPERIENCE, QUALIFICATIONS AND PERSONAL ATTRIBUTES 5+ years of experience as a Creative Director, Associate Creative Director, or equivalent creative leadership role. Demonstrated experience developing campaigns for luxury hospitality, travel, or premium lifestyle brands . Strong portfolio showcasing both motion and still photography campaigns. Experience collaborating directly with clients, agencies, and cross-functional creative teams. Comfortable leading creative development from concept through production and post-production. Deep understanding of luxury visual storytelling, art direction, and brand building. Proven ability to balance creative ambition with production realities. Excellent presentation, communication, and creative leadership skills. Strong eye for design, photography, cinematography, and visual culture. Experience working on location and supporting creative execution during production is highly preferred. Enthusiastic, positive, and able to work well under pressure. ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------- EQUAL EMPLOYMENT OPPORTUNITY STATEMENT Underhill is an Equal Opportunity Employer and Prohibits Discrimination and Harassment of Any Kind: Underhill is committed to the principle of equal employment opportunity for all employees and to providing employees with a work environment free of discrimination and harassment. All employment decisions at Underhill are based on business needs, job requirements and

title: Founding Head of Performance
description: Founding Head of Performance Creative & Content for GutRx Physician-Led Health, Wellness & Performance Marketing Company: GutRx Location: Remote Commitment: Full-time founding-team position Reports to: Founders Equity Compensation: 3%–7% ownership opportunity Travel: Occasional travel for physician, product, and lifestyle shoots About GutRx GutRx is a physician-led digestive-health company focused on helping consumers better understand and support their gut health. Our content features Dr. Raj and Dr. Fierro, whose medical expertise allows GutRx to educate consumers, address common digestive-health concerns, and build trust through credible, approachable information. GutRx is building a differentiated health brand at the intersection of physician credibility, consumer education, compelling content, and educational marketing. You will turn the expertise of Dr. Raj and Dr. Fierro into compelling content for paid advertising, organic social media, email, landing pages, Amazon, retail, and other digital channels. This is not a traditional Social Media Manager or Videographer role. It is a hands-on founding position combining: Creative strategy Direct-response copywriting Physician interviewing and direction Trend and audience research Video production and editing Performance analysis Content operations Brand and regulatory judgment You will be expected to create the systems, processes, creative standards, and testing infrastructure needed to make content a meaningful growth engine for GutRx. Compensation GutRx is offering an equity award intended to represent 3%–5% of the company’s fully diluted ownership immediately following the grant . Establish the company’s creative operating system, production calendar, testing roadmap, and reporting structure. Translate GutRx’s positioning and product benefits into creative concepts designed to earn attention and drive measurable action. Identify the most important customer problems, objections, motivations, questions, and purchasing triggers. Monitor platform trends, competitor activity, customer reviews, comments, emerging gut-health conversations, and successful creative from adjacent categories. Determine which trends GutRx should adapt and which would undermine the company’s medical credibility. Develop content for different stages of the customer journey, from initial awareness through conversion, retention, and advocacy. Build several recognizable, repeatable content featuring Dr. Raj and Dr. Fierro. Physician Content Development Work closely with Dr. Raj and Dr. Fierro to identify relevant, timely, and commercially useful content topics. Research topics and prepare the physicians before each filming session. Interview the physicians and extract clear, concise, engaging explanations. Translate technical medical information into language consumers can readily understand. Write scripts, outlines, interview questions, talking points, headlines, captions, hooks, and calls to action. Create multiple openings and variations for every major concept. Help the physicians communicate naturally without making the content feel scripted or promotional. title: Creative Strategist — Direct Response Video Ads (Remote)
description: CREATIVE STRATEGIST Direct Response Video Ads | Health & Beauty Remote | Full-Time | US Eastern Hours Opportunity Overview We’re a DTC subscription brand doing ~$2.5M/month across ~45,000 active subscribers. Our products help women with hair, anti-aging, and skincare. We sell primarily through Meta ads, with native, YouTube, TikTok, and Google as secondary channels. Creative is our #1 growth lever. Full stop. We need a Creative Strategist who can produce 13–20 net unique video ad concepts per week, manage the QA pipeline, and help us scale winning creative across four product funnels. This is not a brand advertising role. This is direct response. If you don’t know what a hook rate is, this isn’t for you. If you think “creative strategy” means mood boards and brand guidelines, keep scrolling. What You’ll Actually Do • Write 13–20 video ad scripts per week across multiple product funnels (hair, anti-aging, gummies, skincare). You’ll work alongside one senior strategist who’s currently carrying all four funnels solo. Your job is to take half that load and run with it. Every concept must be a net unique—different format, different angle, different structure. We don’t want five variations of the same talking head. We want a UGC ad, a podcast clip, an AI avatar interview, a skit, an ingredient animation, a street interview—real creative range. • Concept, script, and QA short-form video ads (1–3 minutes) for Meta, native, and YouTube. You own the creative from idea to launch-ready asset. That means spying on what’s working across verticals, pulling inspiration from outside your niche, and building briefs that give editors everything they need to execute. • QA is 50% of this job. Frame-by-frame review of every video before it goes live. You’ll send ads back to editors 2–3 times until they’re perfect. Caption sizing, image mismatches, pacing, transitions, audio sync—nothing ships unless it’s right. If you’re not the type of person who notices a 2px caption offset or a 0.5-second pacing issue, this role will eat you alive. • Manage a team of 3–5 video editors (Philippines and Brazil-based) and coordinate with creator/UGC talent. You give clear briefs with specific format instructions, set deadlines, track revision rates, and hold people accountable to output and quality standards. Editors get lazy—your job is to keep standards high and never let slop through. • Spy relentlessly. The best creative strategists are obsessive about knowing what’s working—not just in our vertical, but across all of direct response. You’re scrolling organic content daily, combing through ad libraries and spy tools, and pulling formats and structures from other verticals to apply to ours. Our best concepts come from finding a validated angle in our space and layering in a format from a completely different vertical. That’s the unlock. • Read performance data and adjust. You’ll have access to ad account dashboards. You need to understand CAC, CPC, CTR, hook rate, and hold rate—and use that data to inform what you write next. If an ad isn’t taking spend, you should be able to diagnose why and pivot the next brief accordingly. • Understand how ad creative connects to funnels. We run advertorial and VSL-style landing pages. The ad’s promise has to match the funnel’s story. You need to understand (or be willing to learn fast) how the full pipeline works from click to conversion. Who We’re Looking For The ideal candidate: •     Has produced winning direct response video ads—ideally in health, supplement, or beauty DTC. You can point to ads you wrote that actually scaled and explain why they worked with real metrics, not vanity numbers. •     Understands hook theory. You know that 90% of ad performance lives in the first 3 seconds. You think hook-first, always. Every brief starts with the hook, not the body copy. •     Knows what a net unique concept is. Every ad you produce should be genuinely different from the last—new formats, new structures, new avatar types, ne
score_reason: Remote + creative scope are strong, but DTC/direct-response video production is a significant step down from Travis's national CD integrated campaign background, and the role reads more like a high-volume creative producer than strategic leadership.

title: Head of Brand Marketing
description: At Salted, we believe the future of restaurants doesn’t look anything like what we see today. The most important brands will be built outside four walls and will find new, innovative ways to build deep relationships with their customers. Currently, Salted is the largest delivery-first restaurant group in the US, with locations in 10 states and 20+ cities. We build, own and operate better-for-you restaurant brands that define how a new generation of customers want to eat. More info on our website. We’re looking for a Head of Brand Marketing who will lead the charge as we turn our brands into household names. The right candidate is a passionate builder, a problem solver, a big thinker, and someone who has a pulse for how brands win their way into not only the culture zeitgeist, but the hearts and minds of customers. Responsibilities: Lead the identity development and storytelling behind our brands, with a primary focus on our flagship brand, moonbowls. Ensure the consistency of our brand messaging and value proposition across all customer touchpoints. Work closely with the CEO and R&D team to guide our culinary roadmap from a brand and market opportunity perspective (ie, How do we best reflect our brand values in our culinary offerings? What are the culinary and cultural trends that map to our brand values and should be on our roadmap?) Own the post-purchase customer journey to maximize order frequency and customer lifetime value. You will spearhead retention strategies, digital loyalty initiatives, and occasion-based campaigns that drive repeat ordering behavior and brand stickiness. TLDR: you will drive evangelism! Develop and manage a holistic strategy for introducing our brands into new markets, including social media and community outreach. Develop high ROI opportunities to collaborate with digital influencers and build community engagement. Direct the editorial strategy across all brand channels, creating a cohesive narrative that boosts community interaction and achieves sales targets. Oversee the external brand communications strategy, shaping public perception with general and targeted media outlets Partner directly with the CEO on high-level strategic planning and thought leadership initiatives. Requirements: Track record of making brands pop. Builder mentality with a deep passion for storytelling. Excellent communication skills and attention to detail. Deep understanding of customer behavior, cultural trends, and storytelling across paid, owned, and earned channels. Demonstrated success launching breakthrough campaigns, driving revenue growth, and building brand equity. Experience managing teams and working collaboratively across an organization. Experience working at a high growth company is a big plus. Salary Range: $150k-$220k Benefits: Medical, Dental, & Vision Plans 401k with up to a 4% employer match after one year Unlimited PTO Remote Work (but big plus if you are located in or willing to relocate to LA) Paid Family Leave To Apply: If you feel you would be a good fit for this role, please send an email to jobs@hellosalted.com with "Head of Brand Marketing" in the subject line. Include your resumé and a very short note on why you think there might be the right person. We read every email.
score_reason: Senior brand leadership role with strong creative scope, but DTC/QSR focus and no explicit remote/location flexibility mentioned—need clarification on work arrangement and geographic requirements.
